A LAW PARTNERSHIP

LOS ANGELES OFFICE
ONE WILSHIRE BOULEVARD
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 9017-5321

FRESNO, CALIFORNIA 93711-3001
(209) 233-1500

STUEART STREET TOWER, SUITE 1300
ONE MARKET PLAZA
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94105-1402

SACRAMENTO OFFICE
SUITE 310
1111 "L" STREET
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814-3958
(916) 442-1300

TELEPHONE (415) 281-2000
FACSIMILE (415) 281-2010

ELVON MUSICK 1890-1968
LEROY A. GARRETT 1906-1963
JOSEPH D. PEELER 1895-1991

WRITER'S DIRECT DIAL NUMBER

September 4, 1992                    (415) 281-2020


Scott Hallabrin
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

               Re:  Marin Hospital District
                    Annual Report to the Community

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     This office represents the Marin Hospital District (the
"District"), a California local hospital district as defined in
Health and Safety Code §§32000 et seq. The purpose of this letter
is to request an advisory letter from the Commission with respect
to an "Annual Report to the Community" proposed for dissemination
to District residents.

     The District was established in 1948, and opened the
Marin General Hospital (the "Hospital Facility") in 1952. In 1985,
the District entered into a thirty year lease with Marin General
Hospital, a California nonprofit public benefit corporation
("MGH"), whereunder the District leased to MGH the premises and
buildings comprising the Hospital Facility.  In addition, the
District transferred certain assets and liabilities of the Hospital
Facility to MGH. As a result of these transactions, MGH now
operates the Hospital Facility for the benefit of District
residents, subject to the reversion of the Hospital Facility to the
District upon expiration of the Lease.

     The District remains in operation as a landlord of the
Hospital Facility, and for the purposes of monitoring MGH's
compliance with its lease obligations and promoting health care
services within the District. The District does not collect tax
revenues, provide direct health care services or have any
employees. All of the District's revenues are derived from rent
paid by MGH or from interest earned on unspent District funds.

MUSICK, PEELER & GARRETT
A LAW PARTNERSHIP


Scott Hallabrin
September 4, 1992
Page 2

     In February, 1992, the District Board authorized the
Chair of the Board to prepare a draft Annual Report to the
Community and to bring the draft Report back to the Board for
review and a final decision at its August, 1992 Board meeting.
Prior to this year, the Board has not published or disseminated an
Annual Report.  However, the action of the Board at its February
meeting assumed that the Board would publish Annual Reports in the
future.

     On August 25, 1992, the District Board authorized legal
counsel to submit an outline of the draft Report to the Commission
for an advisory letter determining whether the Report and its form
of dissemination comply with Commission regulations governing
"newsletters" and "mass mailings" sent at public expense (See,
Government code §89001; Title 2, Cal. Code of Regs., §1801).

     Enclosed is an outline of the draft Report.  The Report
will either be mailed to all residents of the District or published
as an advertisement or insert in one or more local newspapers
within the District.  The final version of the Report will not
contain the names or photographs of the members of the District
Board.  It will be accompanied by a cover letter which will
generally describe the contents of the Report.

     If there any questions, or if additional information is
required or helpful in issuing an advisory letter, please do not
hesitate to contact the undersigned.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        M. Steven Lipton
                                        for MUSICK, PEELER & GARRETT

MSL:msl
Enclosure
cc:  District Board Members

A1232876

REPORT TO THE COMMUNITY

    D. Lease Compliance System

            (a) Utilization of hospital services

            (c) Debt service & rental payments

    D. Capital Investment in Hospital

        2. Cancer Center

        2. Number of Employees

    A. Crisis in Health Funding and Access

    B. District Revenues and Expenses

    C. Collaborative Efforts Combining Public/private Resources

A3340427                                    -3-

MARIN HOSPITAL DISTRICT
P. O. Box 8010                    San Rafael, CA 94912-8010                    415-925-7000

September 10, 1992

Scott Hallabrin
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804

                          Re:  Marin Hospital District
                               Annual Report to the Community

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     I am writing to you in connection with a request made to the
FPPC for an opinion.  The request was made on September 4, 1992 by
Steve Lipton, Esq. of Musick, Peeler & Garrett, on behalf of the
Marin Hospital District.

     I am a Board member of the Marin Hospital District.  As a
Board member I opposed distribution of a "Report" from the District
which was political, subjective, and misrepresented hospital fi-
nancial information.  I advised the Board that I would file a law-
suit against the Board if this Report went out in its present form
and prior to the November 1992 election for the Hospital District.

     I believe that the "Report" is "political" for the following
reasons: (1) It's sole author, Board Member Miller, refused to
distribute the Report after the November election, at which election
there will be a hotly contested race which could change the majority
faction of the Board.  There has been no reason given for this "rush
to publish", after 7 years of no reports; (2) The Report states
facts which are not true in an effort to misrepresent conduct of the
corporation which operates the hospital (which information the
District Board cannot even verify), in order to support the candidacy
of two Hospital District candidates, who are supported by the ma-
jority faction of the Board and the hospital operation corporate
authorities; (3) The Report dovetails the published election state-
ments of the candidates supported by the District Board majority
faction; (4) The Report will cost $25,000 to distribute and it adds

Page Two
Scott Hallabrin
September 10, 1992

virtually nothing to the five Reports issued by the hospital operating
corporation, which had to have spent at least $75,000 on such publi-
cations. Also, the Report is replete with half truths or old facts,
which misrepresent the present circumstances of the hospital.

Secondly, in the District Board Chairman's haste to support the
candidacy of two hand picked candidates, she has included in the
"Annual Report" financial information which is factually untrue and
misrepresents the Hospital's finances and charitable support of the
community. Such financial reports are not relevant to the District
Board's obligations or operation, and cannot be verified. Such
irresponsible "reporting" by the Board constitutes a breach of our
fiduciary duty to the District taxpayers, particularly in view of
the recent IRS guidelines for determining tax-exempt status of hospi-
tals. Several hospitals recently have lost their tax-exempt status
due in part to misrepresentations about the amount of charity care
provided.

For these reasons, particularly the suspect "rush" timing of
the distribution of the subject "Annual Report", the publication and
mailing of this report using public funds, violates FPPC guidelines
and rules; constitutes an improper use of public funds for political
purposes; and constitutes a misrepresentation of facts by an elected
board which is violation of the Board's fiduciary duty.

Finally, and significantly, this "Annual Report" fails to mention
the fact that in this Legislative Session the Assem and Senate
overwhelmingly passed urgency legislation which prohibits the kind of
transfers of public assets which took place at Marin General Hospital,
which transfer was approved by the Hospital District Board. The legisla-
tion was passed after consideration of the abuses which have occured
at "reorganized" hospitals such as Marin General.

Nevertheless, ignoring that legislative mandate, members of this
Hospital District Board chose to deceive the Marin public by spending
$25,000 to make laudatory statements about our lessee hospital oper-
ating corporation.

I submit that the proposed "Annual Report" is political and it
misrepresents material facts, and that the FPPC should make such a
finding. At the very least, the FPPC should recommend that publication

Page Three
Scott Hallabrin
September 10, 1992


of the first annual report made in seven years, should not occur un-
til after the November 6, 1992 election, as there is no business
necessity or any non-political reason for publication and distribu-
tion before that date.

Very truly yours,

NANCY P. McCARTHY
